title: Fix SQL Query to Extract Unique Product Categories without Duplicate Sorting

statement.markdown: You are given a table `products` with columns `id`, `name`, and `category`. Write a SQL query to extract unique product categories without any duplicate sorting. The query should return each category only once, sorted in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a SQL query string.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a SQL query string.
statement.notes: The query should be optimized for performance.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the products table

examples.input: SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY COUNT(category) DESC;
examples.output: SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY category;
examples.explanation: The original query is sorting by the count of each category in descending order, while the correct query should return each category only once in ascending order.

examples.input: SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY NULL;
examples.output: SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY category;
examples.explanation: The original query is sorting by NULL, while the correct query should return each category only once in ascending order.

hints.text: Think about how to remove duplicate sorting in the SQL query.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The correct query should use the GROUP BY clause to group the products by category, and then use the ORDER BY clause to sort the categories in ascending order.
editorial.bruteForce: One approach is to use a subquery to remove duplicates and then sort the result.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use the GROUP BY clause with the ORDER BY clause to sort the categories directly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the GROUP BY clause with the ORDER BY clause to sort the categories in ascending order without any duplicates.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the correct query will return each category only once, sorted in ascending order.
commonMistakes: Sorting by count of each category

starterCode.javascript: var query = "SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY COUNT(category) DESC;";

buggyStarterCode.javascript: var query = "SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY COUNT(category) DESC;";

solutions.javascript:
// Solution
var query = "SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY category;";

testCases.output: SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY category;

testCases.input: SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY NULL;
testCases.output: SELECT category FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY category;
